event: Saint Benedict reveals to monks what they have eaten in secret
symbols: table with food — the occasion of the monks' secret indulgence
Benedict's authoritative gesture — prophetic knowledge revealing hidden sin
monks' postures of surprise — conscience exposed before spiritual authority
monastic setting — the rule of life and obedience to the abbot
rocky landscape — the austere wilderness of monastic asceticism
description: A fresco set within a painted arched frame, depicting a scene from monastic life. Several robed figures are gathered around a table in an outdoor or semi-outdoor setting with rocky terrain and vegetation. A central standing figure in monastic robes appears to be addressing or confronting the seated figures at the table. The scene includes additional figures to the left, some in hooded robes. A Latin inscription runs along the bottom of the fresco. The landscape behind shows rugged cliffs and sparse trees.

_meta.title: Life of St. Benedict. Benedict Tells Two Monks What They Have Eaten
